title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Browser and Node Client Systems · Type Recall
summary: A design-system maintainer reconstructing an offline failure handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for cookies, credential modes, api keys, bearer tokens, and secret hygiene, producing least-authority credential and session policy. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch least-authority credential and session policy after a delayed changed case. Learne…
objectives: Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Recognize ambient cookie authority and require CSRF defenses for state-changing browser requests. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Place API keys in approved headers or signing inputs and exclude them from URLs, source, telemetry, and error text. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Cookie Jar Scope · changed case · Review Cookie Jar Scope
steps.instruction:
review case — offline design-system client handles host-only session cookie SID-5, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently. Define jar isolation, CSRF, scope, rotation, redaction, and redirect boundaries. Competency probe: Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Which action demonstrates Cookie Jar Scope without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Cookie Jar Scope · changed case · Review Cookie Jar Scope.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, secure, httponly, and samesite scope through a deliberate jar policy. demonstrate the result through typescript 7 strict types plus whatwg fetch request, response, headers, url, streams, and abortsignal contracts. For Fetch and TypeScript Cookie Jar Scope · changed case · Review Cookie Jar Scope, keep Cookie Jar Scope connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
